title: Maternal Mortality Facility Delivery And Emergency Obstetric Care Access In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where maternal mortality risk is highest and emergency obstetric care access is lowest in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking DHS delivery data, health facility location data, and travel-time estimates at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Sub-Saharan Africa accounts for roughly two-thirds of global maternal deaths, with lifetime risk of maternal death exceeding 1 in 40 in several countries versus 1 in 12,000 in high-income countries. The majority of deaths occur from preventable causes — hemorrhage, sepsis, eclampsia — for which timely access to emergency obstetric care is the single most effective intervention. But verified facility-level EmOC readiness data and travel-time maps that could guide ambulance placement and referral protocols are incomplete.

success_metrics.target: Every EmOC access estimate links facility location, service readiness, and travel-time data with methodology.
No health-system investment recommendation merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status and review records.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
